note: Aggregator (ex-core, now contrib) ingests RSS/RDF/Atom feeds into aggregator_feed / aggregator_item content entities via a 3-stage fetcher/parser/processor plugin pipeline run on cron. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: In the Drupal Aggregator module, what two content entity types does it define, and what does each represent?
evals.expected_output: aggregator_feed (a subscribed source: title, url, refresh interval) and aggregator_item (an individual article imported from a feed). Both are content entities.

evals.prompt: Aggregator imports feeds through three swappable plugin types. Name them, their order, and which interface each implements.
evals.expected_output: Order: fetcher (FetcherInterface, downloads raw data) → parser (ParserInterface, converts to a common item structure) → processor (ProcessorInterface, acts on/stores items). One fetcher and one parser are active; processors can be multiple.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what is the configure route and admin path for the Aggregator global settings form, and where do you add/list feeds?
evals.expected_output: Settings: route aggregator.admin_settings at /admin/config/services/aggregator/settings. Feeds are added/listed at the overview /admin/config/services/aggregator (aggregator.admin_overview).

evals.prompt: Which permissions does Aggregator define, what does each gate, and what security caution applies to the admin one?
evals.expected_output: 'access news feeds' = view /aggregator and feeds/items; 'administer news feeds' = manage feeds/settings (restrict access). Caution: feed creation can be used for SSRF (scan localhost / reach intranet feeds), so grant admin only to trusted roles.

evals.prompt: How does Aggregator actually pull new items from feeds over time on a Drupal site? Describe the cron/queue mechanism.
evals.expected_output: hook_cron() queues each feed whose refresh interval has elapsed into the 'aggregator_feeds' queue; the AggregatorRefresh queue worker then runs Feed::refreshItems() → ItemsImporter::refresh() (fetch→parse→process).

evals.prompt: In PHP on Drupal 11, how do I force an immediate re-import of a single aggregator feed without waiting for cron? Which service and method?
evals.expected_output: Load the aggregator_feed entity and call the aggregator.items.importer service (ItemsImporter): $importer->refresh($feed). ItemsImporter::delete($feed) removes its stored items.

evals.prompt: I need Aggregator to download feeds through a custom authenticated HTTP client. What kind of plugin do I write, which attribute/interface, and where does it live?
evals.expected_output: Write a fetcher plugin: a class in src/Plugin/aggregator/fetcher/ annotated with the #[AggregatorFetcher(id, title)] attribute, implementing FetcherInterface::fetch(FeedInterface $feed). It then becomes selectable on the settings form.

evals.prompt: Without registering a new plugin, how can a module swap the class used for Aggregator's default parser?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_aggregator_parser_info_alter(array &$info) and set $info['aggregator']['class'] to your class. Parallel hooks exist for fetcher and processor.

evals.prompt: How do I stop Aggregator from accumulating unlimited old items, and which config key controls it?
evals.expected_output: The 'Discard items older than' age is aggregator.settings items.expire (seconds; default 9676800). It is contributed by the default processor; set it via the settings form or drush config:set. Use FeedStorageInterface::CLEAR_NEVER to keep items forever.

evals.prompt: Is Aggregator a Drupal core module? What dependencies and external library does the contrib version need?
evals.expected_output: It was a core module, deprecated in core 9.4 and removed in 10.0, now maintained in contrib. Depends on core file, filter, options and requires the laminas/laminas-feed library.
